jokes.text: God! Sorry! Sorry! What's happening? I can't hear! And I'm dying!
jokes.setup: Ben tries to surprise Leslie with something loud
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overreaction to what's presumably music or noise
jokes.explanation: Ben's characteristic overdramatic reaction to sensory overload. Good economy in the escalating panic.

jokes.text: Well, this just keeps getting weirder.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Ben to prom via cake surprise
jokes.punchline: Ben's dry assessment of the escalating absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben Wyatt reaction - understated, logical response to Leslie's characteristic over-the-top gesture.

jokes.text: Which reminds me, I need to learn Latin.
jokes.setup: Leslie volunteers to teach Latin to help the school
jokes.punchline: The casual admission she doesn't actually know Latin
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie Knope overcommitment followed by reality check. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: A few weeks later, he almost died from a VCR electrocution. Legend has it that he can still turn on a microwave just by blinking at it.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions making out with Harvey from A.V. Club
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous consequences of his electrocution
jokes.explanation: Beautifully escalated absurdity with the microwave detail. Creative and unexpected.

jokes.text: Well, I thought you'd never ask, because we're nearing 40.
jokes.setup: Leslie formally asks Ben to prom
jokes.punchline: Ben pointing out the age inappropriateness
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about their age relative to prom attendance.

jokes.text: Aw, did you want to DJ, little puppy? I didn't know that little puppies could operate an iPod with their little puppy paws.
jokes.setup: Ben shows interest in DJing
jokes.punchline: Tom's condescending puppy metaphor
jokes.explanation: Very Tom Haverford - condescending but in an absurdly childish way.

jokes.text: Eew and boo.
jokes.setup: Tom's puppy insults
jokes.punchline: Ben's flat, unimpressed response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben response - economical, dry, and appropriately dismissive.

jokes.text: I had a show called 'Zoot Suit Wyatt' on Carleton College radio. Tuesdays from 3:00 to 5:00 A.M., I was the king of swing.
jokes.setup: Ben reveals he used to DJ
jokes.punchline: The embarrassingly specific details of his swing radio show
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cringey Ben backstory with great specific details. 'Zoot Suit Wyatt' is memorably embarrassing.

jokes.text: I thought we as a culture agreed to forget the year that everyone was into swing.
jokes.setup: Ben's swing DJ revelation
jokes.punchline: Tom's cultural commentary on the swing revival
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about cultural trends. Good observational comedy.

jokes.text: Are those jense-trodder color tabs? I thought those were discontinued. They were. I had to order them through some Mexican back channels. Juan Julio Oficina Supplies? I thought they went out of business. They did, but they opened up a new one in Oaxaca.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Allison discuss office supplies
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and absurd supply chain knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating bureaucrat nerd-out. Shows Leslie finding her organizational soulmate through absurd specificity.

jokes.text: What is happening right now?
jokes.setup: Leslie and Allison's office supply conversation
jokes.punchline: Ben's bewildered normal-person response
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to the absurdity. Ben as audience surrogate.

jokes.text: Because every single song you own is a banger? Every song I download has to pass a series of rigorous tests to answer one simple question: Is it a banger?
jokes.setup: Ben questions Tom's no-setlist approach
jokes.punchline: Tom's overly scientific approach to determining 'bangers'
jokes.explanation: Peak Tom Haverford - treating pop music selection with scientific rigor. Character-perfect.

jokes.text: I once accidentally downloaded a Lumineers song. I had to throw away my whole computer just to be safe.
jokes.setup: Tom's strict music standards
jokes.punchline: The extreme measures taken to avoid acoustic instruments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and perfect Tom character moment. The computer destruction is memorably excessive.

jokes.text: I hope that high school auditorium's big enough, 'cause I'm bringing 10,000 maniacs.
jokes.setup: Ben talks about music flow and curation
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun on the band name
jokes.explanation: Dad joke that's perfect for Ben's character but predictable in structure.

jokes.text: Sometimes it's really hard to be friends with you.
jokes.setup: Ben's terrible 10,000 Maniacs pun
jokes.punchline: Tom's exasperated honest response
jokes.explanation: Tom serving as straight man to Ben's dad jokes is a nice role reversal.

jokes.text: DJ Robo Drop.
jokes.setup: Tom announces his new DJ partnership
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously over-the-top DJ name
jokes.explanation: Very Tom - unnecessarily dramatic DJ persona. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Or, no, we can't, because they moved to prison.
jokes.setup: Leslie reminisces about after-party at Megan's house where parents let them drink
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that the permissive parents are now incarcerated
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy subversion. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Prom is nothing but a huge party full of smiling, dancing people enjoying themselves. It's literally my worst nightmare.
jokes.setup: Context of prom planning
jokes.punchline: April's characteristic hatred of joy and celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect April Ludgate moment - finding the horrible in something universally considered fun.

jokes.text: ♪ Sarah Wei--April Ludgate ♪
jokes.setup: Andy sings a romantic song from high school
jokes.punchline: He forgets to change the name mid-song and has to correct it awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy moment - well-meaning but bumbling execution. The name fumble is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Fine. I'll go with you, because Leslie's making us, and we live together, and we only have one car right now because you laked mine.
jokes.setup: Andy asks April to prom
jokes.punchline: The escalating practical reasons ending with the invented verb 'laked'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from romantic to practical, and 'laked' is a perfect neologism that immediately makes sense.

jokes.text: I tried to jump it over a lake.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what 'laked' means
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact explanation of the absurd car destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an absurd explanation. Very April to be matter-of-fact about something ridiculous.

jokes.text: Is there some secret society for the greatest humans on earth? Do you meet Tom Hanks and Elena Kagan in the Statue of Liberty's crown?
jokes.setup: Leslie discovers Ron and Allison know each other
jokes.punchline: The elaborate conspiracy theory with specific celebrity examples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie spiral into conspiracy. The specific celebrity choices and location make it memorable.

jokes.text: God, this fake club I'm inventing is amazing.
jokes.setup: Leslie's own conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Her immediate self-awareness and appreciation of her own creation
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment where Leslie appreciates her own wild imagination. Good self-aware comedy.

jokes.text: That's what he calls city hall.
jokes.setup: Ron's insulting description of city hall
jokes.punchline: Allison's matter-of-fact clarification
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective clarification joke. Establishes Allison's familiarity with Ron's ways.

jokes.text: Why don't you shut your mustache?
jokes.setup: Ron gives practical career advice
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical but specific insult targeting his facial hair
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's absurd but targeted. Good economy and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Oh, really? Is it worth a few bucks to get a million splinters in your fingers and then cut off your fingers accidentally, 'cause that will happen.
jokes.setup: Allison says she needs money for school
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly dire predictions about sawmill work
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Leslie in competition mode. Dark but in character.

jokes.text: I once did him a favor. I built his sawmill.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions getting Allison a job through a favor
jokes.punchline: The massive understatement of building an entire sawmill as a 'favor'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - massive understatement delivered deadpan. The misdirection from 'favor' to 'built his sawmill' is brilliant.

jokes.text: And if Ron tries to stop me, he can eat a big ol' bowl of butts.
jokes.setup: Leslie's determination to recruit Allison
jokes.punchline: The childish but emphatic threat
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - trying to be fierce but sounding like a child. The phrase is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: If you don't show up in a limo, legally, they can't let you in.
jokes.setup: Someone questions paying for a limo
jokes.punchline: Andy's confident but completely false legal claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - confidently stating something absurd as if it's legal fact.

jokes.text: I'm April's mom.
jokes.setup: Someone asks who Orin is
jokes.punchline: April deadpan claiming to be her own mother
jokes.explanation: Absurd deadpan delivery typical of April. The visual of her saying this to confused onlookers adds to it.

jokes.text: How am I supposed to keep my Husker Du albums in near-mint condish?
jokes.setup: Ben complains about lack of proper equipment
jokes.punchline: The overly precious concern for obscure indie albums and saying 'condish'
jokes.explanation: Good Ben nerdiness with the abbreviation 'condish' making it extra pretentious.

jokes.text: Hey, I have an idea. Why don't you throw them in the garbage?
jokes.setup: Ben's precious concern for his vinyl
jokes.punchline: Tom's dismissive suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good Tom response to Ben's pretentiousness. Sharp and economical.

jokes.text: My date was pretty pissed, but, uh, I looked fly as hell.
jokes.setup: Tom was late to his own prom
jokes.punchline: His priorities being completely backwards but delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Peak Tom Haverford - valuing appearance over everything else, including his date's feelings.

jokes.text: Oh, did he? I didn't even notice. No bigs.
jokes.setup: Leslie gets an A-plus from the principal
jokes.punchline: Her obvious lie about not caring while clearly obsessed
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie trying to play it cool while being obviously thrilled about academic validation.

jokes.text: Stop wasting helium. It is intended for welding and filling air ships.
jokes.setup: Context of balloon preparations
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical and old-fashioned view of helium use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - practical to the point of comedy, with the anachronistic 'air ships' reference.

jokes.text: I was 12 years old. Never went again. Felt like I had outgrown it.
jokes.setup: Ron describes attending prom once
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he was 12 and his matter-of-fact assessment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment. The casual revelation of going to prom at 12 followed by his typical understated wisdom is perfect.

jokes.text: Like flowers on the sunset of an eagle's poetry.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to describe the beauty of youth
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical but poetic-sounding metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy poetry - sounds profound but makes no logical sense. Memorably absurd.

jokes.text: It's just--it's not good. It's not a good movie.
jokes.setup: Andy's elaborate story about watching Expendables 2 multiple times
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic conclusion that it's simply bad
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection. Sets up expectation that he'll find hidden value, then deflates it completely.

jokes.text: Um, I'm gonna go to the girls' bathroom to check the trash cans for babies,
jokes.setup: April wants to avoid dancing
jokes.punchline: The casually disturbing excuse she makes up
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark April humor. The casual delivery of something so disturbing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: The stuff you're playing is so old, it's probably trinking on grizzledump.
jokes.setup: Tom tries to play music for teenagers
jokes.punchline: The completely made-up slang that sounds plausible
jokes.explanation: Creative invented slang that sounds believable. Good satire of generational language gaps.

jokes.text: What's grizzledump, Ben? What's grizzledump?
jokes.setup: Tom doesn't understand the teen slang
jokes.punchline: His desperate, panicked repetition
jokes.explanation: Good panic moment for Tom realizing he's out of touch. The repetition adds desperation.

jokes.text: Congratulations, Ron. You've ruined prom.
jokes.setup: Ron and Leslie's competition confuses Allison
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic overstatement of the consequences
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie dramatics. The overstatement is in character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Like I'm gonna dance with a bunch of kids while he's coaching Duke to the final four.
jokes.setup: Donna explains why she didn't go to her prom
jokes.punchline: Her very specific and practical age-gap concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - practical and direct about her dating preferences. The Duke reference is very specific.

jokes.text: How can I love someone when I hate everything he loves?
jokes.setup: April watches Andy enjoying prom
jokes.punchline: Her genuine existential relationship crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for April - the genuine concern beneath her cynicism.

jokes.text: First of all, your telephone is ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions calling on his mobile telephone
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dismissive comment about his presumably old phone
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective dig at Ron's old-fashioned technology choices.

jokes.text: I wouldn't say 'harassing' so much as persistently tormenting.
jokes.setup: Principal accuses Leslie of harassing Allison
jokes.punchline: Leslie's attempt at a defense that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie logic - trying to defend herself but making it worse. The word choice escalates the crime.

jokes.text: And if this is the evening you decide to have sex, use protection, please!
jokes.setup: Leslie addressing teenagers at prom
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate maternal advice to students
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie cringe moment showing her inability to maintain appropriate boundaries

jokes.text: I was buried in it.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about April's dress
jokes.punchline: The casual implication that she's wearing a burial dress
jokes.explanation: Peak April darkness delivered with perfect economy. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: and hiding mops so the janitor would think he was going insane.
jokes.setup: April describes her high school experience
jokes.punchline: The specific cruel prank she committed
jokes.explanation: Good dark April humor - the specific cruelty makes it memorable and in-character.

jokes.text: I think the extra length could help us get us over that lake.
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting they use the limo for three more hours
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous lake-jumping attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Andy hasn't learned from previous car destruction

jokes.text: like for example the incident with the girls' soccer uniforms back in '05... They wrote all the team names in pink. Nobody told them to do that.
jokes.setup: Ben explains his rating system for Leslie's behavior
jokes.punchline: The specific example of Leslie's past overreach
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory that feels authentic to Leslie's controlling nature.

jokes.text: Blueprints for the future are a fool's errand. They're like blueprints for a house. Nice to have, but any foreman with half a brain doesn't need to look at them.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about planning for the future
jokes.punchline: Ron's metaphor that contradicts itself - blueprints are like blueprints
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron wisdom that's logically circular but somehow makes sense. The metaphor being literal is the joke.

jokes.text: Damn it. I just wish once you would say something stupid so I could ignore it.
jokes.setup: Ron giving Leslie unexpectedly wise advice
jokes.punchline: Leslie's frustration that Ron's wisdom prevents dismissal
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment acknowledging Ron's wisdom while expressing frustration about it

jokes.text: I broke the box, and I threw away all the votes, and I voted for you a thousand times.
jokes.setup: April explaining how Andy became prom king
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of elaborate election fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - matter-of-fact description of criminal activity done for love

jokes.text: He's 33, and I'm 47/immortal.
jokes.setup: Student asks how old they are after being called out
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact claim to be immortal
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response - treating immortality as casually as age. The slash notation adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: I rigged an election and got kicked out of prom. I can get on board with that.
jokes.setup: Andy saying April is having fun now
jokes.punchline: April's approval of criminal behavior as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - finding joy only in subversive illegal activities

jokes.text: Might as well start wearing a plaid shirt and a tie and khakis and whatever shoes you're wearing.
jokes.setup: Tom laments being old and uncool
jokes.punchline: His description perfectly matches Ben's current outfit
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback to Ben's characteristically uncool wardrobe. Tom's unconscious roasting.

jokes.text: Yeah. You're old as shit.
jokes.setup: Ben consoles Tom about being old
jokes.punchline: Tom's blunt agreement that turns the comfort back on Ben
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where Tom's gratitude becomes an insult. Economical and in character.

jokes.text: Greg Pikitis.
jokes.setup: Allison introduces her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: The revelation that it's Leslie's nemesis
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes the entire Allison storyline. Great payoff for series fans.

jokes.text: Offer is off the table. She's a terrible person with terrible judgment.
jokes.setup: Leslie discovers Allison is dating her nemesis
jokes.punchline: Her immediate complete reversal of opinion
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Leslie/Ron competition. Her instant 180 is perfectly in character.
